data.title: US suspends licences to ship nuclear plant parts to China — Reuters
data.description:
NEW YORK/SINGAPORE (June 7): The US in recent days suspended licences for nuclear equipment suppliers to sell to China's power plants, according to four people familiar with the matter, as the two countries engage in a damaging trade war.

The suspensions were issued by the US Department of Commerce, the people said, and affect export licences for parts and equipment used with nuclear power plants.

Nuclear equipment suppliers are among a wide range of companies whose sales have been restricted over the past two weeks as the US-China trade war shifted from negotiating tariffs to throttling each other's supply chains. It is unclear whether a Thursday call between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping would affect the suspensions.

The US and China agreed on May 12 to roll back triple digit, tit-for-tat tariffs for 90 days, but the truce between the two biggest economies quickly went south, with the US claiming China reneged on terms related to rare earth elements, and China accusing the US of "abusing export control measures" by warning that using Huawei Ascend AI chips anywhere in the world violated US export controls. After Thursday's call, further talks on key issues were expected.

The US Department of Commerce did not respond to a request for comment on the nuclear equipment restrictions. On May 28, a spokesperson said the department was reviewing exports of strategic significance to China.

"In some cases, Commerce has suspended existing export licences or imposed additional licence requirements while the review is pending," the spokesperson said in a statement. The Chinese embassy in Washington did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

US nuclear equipment suppliers include Westinghouse and Emerson.

Westinghouse, whose technology is used in over 400 nuclear reactors around the world, and Emerson, which provides measurement and other tools for the nuclear industry, did not respond to requests for comment.

The suspensions affect business worth hundreds of millions of dollars, two of the sources said.

They also coincide with Chinese restrictions on critical metals threatening supply chains for manufacturers worldwide, especially America's Big Three automakers.

Reuters could not determine whether the new restrictions were tied to the trade war, or if and how quickly they might be reinstated. Department of Commerce export licences typically run for four years and include authorized quantities and values.

But many new restrictions on exports to China have been imposed in the last two weeks, according to sources, and include licence requirements for a hydraulic fluids supplier for sales to China.

Other licence suspensions went to GE Aerospace for jet engines for China's COMAC aircraft, sources said.

The US also now requires licences to ship ethane to China, as Reuters reported first last week. Houston-based Enterprise Product Partners said Wednesday that its emergency requests to complete three proposed cargoes of ethane to China, totaling some 2.2 million barrels, had not been granted.

Enterprise said a May 23 requirement for a licence to sell butane to China, in addition to the ethane, was subsequently withdrawn. Dallas-based Energy Transfer said it was notified on Tuesday about the new ethane licensing requirement, and planned to apply and file for an emergency authorisation.

Other sectors that have been hit with new restrictions include companies that sell electronic design automation software such as Cadence Design Systems.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: BNP to cut 200 branches by end of 2026 to kick off retail revamp
data.description:
(June 7): BNP Paribas SA chief executive officer Jean-Laurent Bonnafe plans to cut hundreds of domestic retail branches in an effort to lift profitability at a business that has struggled for years.

In a first step, the French lender plans to close around 80 branches this year and another 120 next, according to people familiar with the matter. Headcount in the branch network is expected to decline by 5% a year, while staff would be added in online and phone banking.

Negotiations with labour unions, which were first informed of the strategy for the business in March, are set to continue in the coming days, the people said, asking not to be identified because the information is private. Affected employees will be offered new roles, and BNP Paribas is also counting on natural attrition to avoid layoffs, according to the people.

“We presented in March an ambitious growth plan to trade unions that outlines a strategic roadmap and related investments for our Commercial & Personal Banking in France,” a spokesperson for BNP Paribas said in an emailed statement. “Every employee of the bank has their place in this project.”

Fixing the unit is a key focus for Bonnafe, after he built up the trading business and propelled the asset management arm into the top ranks with the takeover of Axa SA’s investment arm agreed last year. BNP Paribas last year named Isabelle Loc to run the business, which has struggled as local regulations, costly interest rate hedges and the European Central Bank’s decision to no longer remunerate mandatory reserves weighed on earnings.

The unit is now “at a pivotal point,” chief financial officer Lars Machenil said in an interview in February. The “headwinds are basically behind us.”

Loc took over the business after its former head, Marguerite Berard, left BNP Paribas last year and went on to run Dutch lender ABN Amro Bank NV. She is counting on the digital unit Hello bank! as well as its private banking assets in France to grow the business.

BNP currently has around 1,500 branches in France. Under Loc’s plan, some of the business in the branches will be transferred to remote customers centres, where employees will be offered new jobs, the people said. Others may be offered positions in areas such as private banking, they said.

BNP said previously that it will unveil its plan for the business at the end of June. French newspaper Les Echos has reported that about 500 branches may be cut in total by 2030.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Tesla shares gain after US$152b selloff, but Trump-Musk truce uncertain
data.description:
BENGALURU/LONDON (June 6): Tesla shares recouped some losses triggered by a spat between chief executive officer Elon Musk and US President Donald Trump, although a truce appeared uncertain on Friday as a White House official said the president was not keen on talking to his former ally.

The stock rose 4% after a steep fall in the previous session that wiped out US$152 billion (RM643.04 billion) in market value when the world's most powerful man and its richest publicly entered a war of words over the tax and spending bill.

Earlier, a Politico report had said Trump and Musk were likely to talk later in the day. Musk had signalled on X he was open to easing tensions with the president, agreeing with calls for a detente from users on the social media platform.

But Trump told CNN on Friday, "I'm not even thinking about Elon," adding the billionaire has "got a problem".

Tensions escalated on Thursday after Musk stepped up his criticism of Trump's sweeping tax bill, which proposed largely ending the popular US$7,500 EV tax incentive by the end of 2025.

In response, Trump suggested cuts to the government's contracts with Musk's companies, including rocket maker SpaceX.

"It might be a bit too hopeful to think their relationship will ever go back to what it once was, but if cooler heads prevail and the tension eases, that would definitely be a big improvement for Tesla," said Tesla shareholder Matthew Britzman, who is an analyst at Hargreaves Lansdown.

Lofty stock valuation

An open clash with Trump could pose multiple hurdles for Tesla and the rest of Musk's sprawling business empire.

The US Transportation Department regulates vehicle design standards and would have a big say in whether Tesla can mass-produce robotaxis without pedals and steering wheels.

Tesla did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the call.

Tesla stock is down 29.5% this year after a 14% drop on Thursday. Still, the shares trade at 120 times expected earnings, a lofty multiple compared to other automakers and even tech giants such as Nvidia.

The shares have been on a turbulent ride since last July when Musk backed Trump's White House bid. They surged initially as investors bet on less regulatory pressure for robotaxis, but tumbled due to soft sales and brand fallout from Musk's political stance.

Stronger sales from potential Republican customers were expected to offset a liberal boycott, but analysts warned that Musk's recent actions may erode that buffer too.

"With Musk alienating Republicans, there is no one left to prop up consumer sentiment towards Tesla, which could lead towards a full-scale collapse in brand perception," said political strategist Evan Roth Smith, co-founder of Slingshot Strategies.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Yinson, Pavilion REIT, Bursa Malaysia, Omesti, PIE Industrial, 7-Eleven Malaysia, Gadang, Ancom Nylex, Meridian, CIMB, Ho Hup & Ecomate
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Here is a brief recap of business news and corporate announcements that made the headlines on Friday:

New York-based Stonepeak Partners is reportedly in exclusive talks to acquire Yinson Holdings Bhd (KL:YINSON), potentially valuing the company at RM9 billion (US$2.1 billion), making it one of Malaysia’s biggest deals this year. Stonepeak is partnering with Yinson’s founder and largest shareholder, the Lim family, which owns 26.6%, to take the energy infrastructure company private. Discussions are ongoing, with no guarantee a deal will be finalised. — Stonepeak said in exclusive talks for buyout of US$2.1b Yinson — Bloomberg

Pavilion REIT (KL:PAVREIT) has raised about RM360 million from a private placement of 257.14 million shares at RM1.40 each, a 3.9% discount to the volume-weighted average price of the units for the five market days up to and including June 4 of RM1.4572 per unit. The funds will partly finance its RM480 million acquisition of Banyan Tree Kuala Lumpur and Pavilion Hotel Kuala Lumpur, with the remainder covered by bank borrowings. — Pavilion REIT prices private placement at RM1.40 apiece to raise some RM360m

Bank Islam Malaysia Bhd (KL:BIMB) chief financial officer (CFO) Azizan Abd Aziz is leaving the bank to become CFO of Bursa Malaysia Bhd (KL:BURSA), with the appointment effective August 1. As CFO, Azizan will oversee Bursa Malaysia’s finance functions, including financial and management reporting, corporate planning, and budgeting. — Azizan Abd Aziz leaves Bank Islam to become Bursa Malaysia CFO

Information and communication technology services group Omesti Bhd (KL:OMESTI) has proposed a series of corporate exercises — including debt settlements via share issuances, a RM63.12 million rights issue, a RM45.65 million private placement, and a RM200 million capital reduction — aimed at paring down debt, raising funds, and cutting its accumulated losses from RM286.72 million to RM88.52 million. All in all, assuming full exercise of all outstanding warrants and maximum scenarios of each proposed exercise, the exercises will enlarge Omesti’s share base to 3.5 billion shares from the current 540.67 million. — Omesti plans share issuances to settle debt and capital reduction to slash accumulated losses

PIE Industrial Bhd (KL:PIE) has appointed former Globetronics Technology Bhd(KL:GTRONIC)  CEO Datuk Heng Huck Lee, 68, as an independent and non-executive director, as well as a member of its nomination and audit committees, effective immediately. Meanwhile, Lee Cheow Kooi has stepped down from the board and committees to pursue personal interests. — Former Globetronics CEO Heng Huck Lee joins PIE Industrial's board

Tan Sri Mohd Annuar Zaini has been appointed as the new independent and non-executive chairman of 7-Eleven Malaysia Holdings Bhd (KL:SEM), effective immediately. He replaces Datuk Farhash Wafa Salvador, who stepped down from the role on May 30 to pursue other interests. — Annuar Zaini named new chairman of 7-Eleven Malaysia

Gadang Holdings Bhd (KL:GADANG) will incur about RM19 million in additional costs for FY2025 after terminating a joint development agreement with Cyberview Sdn Bhd to develop land inthe Cyber 9 precinct of Cyberjaya. The joint development agreement inked by Gadang's wholly owned Hillstrand Development Sdn Bhd (HDSB) and Cyberview in May 2014, was to develop a 121.49 acre plot within 10 years. All but Phase 4 — comprising sub-phases 4A, 4B and 4C — remained undeveloped. — Gadang faces RM19 mil hit from termination of joint development in Cyberjaya

Ancom Nylex Bhd (KL:ANCOMNY) plans to take full ownership of Nylex (M) Bhd via a selective capital reduction and repayment exercise, offering 5.1 sen per share — or RM5.3 million in total — to minority shareholders. Post-exercise, Ancom Nylex and its unit Rhodemark Development,which together control 42.21% of Nylex, will become the sole shareholders. The offer price is based on Nylex’s adjusted net assets of RM9.17 million over a share base of 179.79 million shares. — Ancom Nylex seeks to take full control of delisted Nylex via capital reduction

Bursa Malaysia has reprimanded property developer Meridian Bhd (KL:MERIDIAN) and fined five directors RM350,000 for delaying disclosure that the group had minimal business activity. Meridian’s 4QFY2023 revenue of RM2.74 million was just 0.93% of its share capital, triggering a disclosure requirement under Bursa rules. The company only announced this 28 days late, after being prompted by the regulator. — Bursa Malaysia fines Meridian CEO, directors RM350,000 for late disclosure of minimal operations

CIMB Group Bhd (KL:CIMB) said shares of its Thai unit, CIMB Thai Bank, were suspended from trading on the Thai stock exchange on June 5 for failing to meet the minimum 15% public shareholding requirement. The group noted that under new rules effective March 2024, non-compliance results in suspension instead of fines; however, it does not plan to increase the bank’s public shareholding. — CIMB says its Thai unit’s shares suspended for not meeting public shareholding rules

Ho Hup Construction Co Bhd (KL:HOHUP), which slipped into Practice Note 17 (PN17) status earlier in April, has defaulted on revolving credit facilities from AmBank Islamic Bhd totalling RM45.3 million. The default occurred due to the company’s inability to meet principal and interest instalments amid cash flow difficulties. Ho Hup plans to initiate discussions with the bank on a settlement proposal. — Ho Hup defaults on RM45.3 mil loan from AmBank Islamic

Furniture maker Ecomate Holdings Bhd (KL:ECOMATE) plans to diversify into ICT solutions by acquiring a 60% stake in computer servicing firm Progressive Computer Systems Sdn Bhd for RM8.4 million.It also plans a one-for-one bonus issue of up to 358.03 million shares and 358.03 million warrants. The warrant exercise price is yet to be determined, but if fully exercised, it could raise about RM222 million. — Ecomate to diversify into ICT solutions, undertake bonus issue

 

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: MCMC says collection of mobile phone data from telcos only to support policymaking, no personal info involved
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has clarified that its demand for mobile phone data from mobile network operators (MNOs) is mainly for the generation of official statistics to support evidence-based policymaking.

No personally identifiable information is accessed, processed or disclosed, MCMC said in a statement on Friday night.

The statistics generated are used to support policymaking in two key sectors: information, communications and technology, and tourism, it said.

The commission noted that MNOs can either process the data securely in-house and provide the anonymised results, or, if lacking in-house processing capabilities, submit the anonymised data directly to MCMC.

“In both cases, no individual subscriber can be identified through the data collected,” MCMC stressed.

The statement was issued in response to reports that the government had sought detailed records of phone calls and internet usage from telecom firms

According to the South China Morning Post, MCMC had demanded detailed call and internet logs for the first three months of this year. Non-compliance would be an offence under the Communications and Multimedia Act, which carries a penalty of a RM20,000 fine or six months’ imprisonment.

MCMC on Friday said the mobile phone data it collects is used strictly for generating official statistics to support evidence-based policymaking in two key areas — namely, the ICT sector and the tourism sector.

For the ICT sector, it produces detailed statistics on mobile broadband usage and penetration across various administrative levels, while for the tourism sector, it tracks visitor numbers and domestic trips.

The commission also noted that extensive engagement had taken place with all MNOs over the past two years to ensure mutual understanding of data requirements, processes, and privacy safeguards.

“This initiative aligns with international best practices. It mirrors similar projects already implemented in countries such as Indonesia and Brazil, where anonymised telecommunications data is used to enhance national statistics while fully safeguarding user privacy,” said MCMC.

Read also:
Malaysian govt demands telcos hand over detailed phone records, internet usage data — report

Edited ByTan Choe Choe & S Kanagaraju

data.title: Petronas conducts thorough review on overall operations to remain competitive, says CEO
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) reiterated that it is conducting a thorough review of its operations, including assets, processes, expenditures, and workforce to ensure it remains competitive and future-ready.

The national oil firm noted that the top-down, enterprise-wide workforce assessment is aimed at aligning the organisation with long-term direction to deliver greater value.

Petronas president and group chief executive officer Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Aziz said Petronas acknowledged that the process might result in difficult but necessary workforce adjustments, with an estimated 10% reduction from its total workforce.

He added that the group is striving to complete the notification process for the affected staff in stages until the end of this year and remains fully committed to managing this process with transparency, care and respect for all impacted employees. 

“The comprehensive review of its assets, processes, expenditures and people is a fundamental shift critical for Petronas to compete and drive future growth amidst a heightening challenging market environment,” Tengku Muhammad Taufik said in a statement. 

To facilitate this exercise, Petronas will implement a general freeze on hiring activities until December 2026, with exceptions made for selected business-critical positions, which will be assessed on a case-by-case basis. 

Read also:
Petronas job cuts to mainly affect contract staff, says PM

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Substantial shareholders countersue Peterlabs over failure to convene EGM to remove directors
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): After Peterlabs Holdings Bhd (KL:PLABS) sued two substantial shareholders to deny an extraordinary general meeting (EGM) request for a board reshuffle, the duo have now countersued the animal health and nutrition product maker and its board.

The two substantial shareholders, Bu Yaw Seng and Datin Lin Ching Yein, who collectively control a stake of more than 10% in Peterlabs, have filed a counterclaim, seeking a declaration that they are entitled to the EGM.

They are also seeking a declaration that Peterlabs’ board members had breached their statutory duty in failing to convene the EGM, and a court order for the EGM be convened.

“The company will defend and resist the counterclaim,” Peterlabs said in a bourse filing on Friday.

Bu and Lin’s counterclaim names Peterlabs’ seven board members and the company as the defendants.

Its board comprises non-executive chairman Datuk Lim Tai Soon, group managing director Lim Tong Seng, executive directors Teo Chin Heng and Yap Siaw Peng, and directors Datuk Ng Boon Siong, Loh Poh Im, and Ho Siew Li.

Peterlabs’ board comprises one more member, namely executive director Datuk Low Saw Foong. However, Low has been suspended from his executive duties pending an internal investigation and an ongoing probe by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission.

Last month, Bu and Lin issued a notice requesting to convene an EGM to remove seven directors and replace them with two nominees.

The notice proposes the removal of six directors — Tai Soon, Tong Seng, Teo, Yap, Boon Siong, and Ho — and alternative director to Boon Siong, Ng Kau.

In their stead, the duo proposed nominating Datuk Wira Pua Kim An and Datuk Seri Garry Chua Kah Seng as new directors of the company.

The existing board members not named among those proposed to be removed by Bu and Lin, are Loh and the suspended Low.

Shares in Peterlabs ended unchanged at 36 sen, valuing the company at RM99.08 million.

Read also:
PeterLabs rejects request to convene EGM to remove directors

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Switzerland hits UBS with US$26b added capital requirement; shares rise
data.description:
BERN (June 6): The Swiss government on Friday proposed stricter rules for UBS following its takeover of Credit Suisse, which could make it hold US$26 billion (RM109.98 billion) more in core capital, confirming some of the bank's worst fears about incoming new regulations.

The key proposal, which the bank would have six to eight years to prepare for after it became law, is that UBS must fully capitalise its foreign units, confirming what many analysts, lawmakers and executives had been expecting.

UBS shares, which have lagged European peers amid uncertainty about the new rules, jumped after the proposals were made public on Friday afternoon, rising by more than 6% and on track for their best day since May 2024.

The government said its capital requirement proposal would allow UBS to reduce its holding of Additional Tier 1 (AT1) bonds by US$8 billion. Today, UBS must only 60% capitalise its foreign units and can cover some of the capital with AT1 debt.

UBS executives say the additional capital burden will put the Zurich-based bank at a disadvantage to rivals and undermine the competitiveness of Switzerland as a financial centre.

Such was the shock in Switzerland over the 2023 collapse of Credit Suisse that top politicians led by Finance Minister Karin Keller-Sutter vowed to introduce more robust rules that would protect taxpayers and prevent another meltdown in future.

Keller-Sutter now holds Switzerland's rotating one-year presidency and Friday's announcement will start a long period of political wrangling over the measures, which the governing federal council called "targeted and proportionate".

"They strengthen trust in the financial centre, which, in the view of the federal council, is central to its stability and competitiveness," the council said in a statement.

A parliamentary inquiry last year noted that since UBS bought Credit Suisse for three billion Swiss francs (US$3.65 billion or RM15.44 billion) in March 2023, it has had a balance sheet bigger than the Swiss economy, and urged the government to take the foreign units into account.

The federal council said it would present drafts on the proposals for consultations with stakeholders in the second half of 2025. Finance Ministry officials say laws requiring parliamentary approval will not enter force before 2028.

Separate measures known as ordinances that can be issued directly by the government could apply from the start of 2027.

A six- to eight-year transition period looked appropriate for UBS to meet new rules on capitalising foreign units from when they come into force, the government said.

That could give the bank until the mid-2030s to comply.

Possible target?

Sources inside the bank have warned the new regulations could make UBS an appealing takeover target.

Under the Swiss proposals, UBS's Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital ratio could end up somewhat higher than those of global rivals, the government said. UBS's CET1 ratio of 14.3% could rise up to 17%, above rivals like JPMorgan at 15.8%, Morgan Stanley at 15.7%, and 15.3% at Goldman Sachs, it said.

Shares in UBS rose more than 60% in the 12 months following its acquisition of Credit Suisse. But the stock has since sharply underperformed; UBS shares have lost about 5% in the past year, while a top European banking index climbed 37%.

Analysts say the new regulations could trigger a rejig of UBS's business model, which now focuses on growth in the US and Asia. To take the edge off the rules, the bank may be tempted to sell some assets, banking experts say.

The Swiss government also set out piecemeal reforms to bolster the market regulator Finma, which was heavily criticised for its response to the Credit Suisse collapse.

These include measures aimed at holding bankers to account, giving the regulator the power to impose fines and making it easier to restrain pay and claw back bonuses. Still, the proposals come years after the European Union introduced similar measures in the wake of the 2007-2009 financial crisis.

The government also proposed making it easier for banks to access liquidity from the Swiss National Bank. Barriers to transferring collateral to the SNB will also be removed.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Wall Street opens higher on jobs data optimism; Tesla rebounds
data.description:
BENGALURU (June 6): US stock indices opened higher on Friday after a strong jobs report calmed worries over the health of the labour market, while Tesla shares rebounded on signs of cooling tensions between CEO Elon Musk and US President Donald Trump.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 312.1 points, or 0.74%, at the open to 42,631.82. The S&P 500 rose 47.8 points, or 0.8%, to 5,987.06, while the Nasdaq Composite rose 228.5 points, or 1.18%, to 19,526.994.

More to come

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Mitrajaya founder Chuah Theong Yee ceases to be substantial shareholder of Eonmetall
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Mitrajaya Group founder Chuah Theong Yee has ceased to be a substantial shareholder in steel products maker Eonmetall Group Bhd (KL:EMETALL).

The cessation came after Chuah sold 410,000 shares representing a 0.13% stake in the company in the open market, Eonmetall's bourse filing on Friday showed.

Based on prior filings, following the disposal, his stake in Eonmetall was trimmed to 4.99% — below the 5% threshold required to be deemed a substantial shareholder.

Chuah emerged as a substantial shareholder in Eonmetall in October 2022 with a 7.3% stake.

He left his stake largely untouched until May this year, when he started trimming his shareholding.

Eonmetall’s largest shareholder is its executive director Datuk Goh Cheng Huat with a 41.08% stake, followed by Magma Group Bhd (KL:MAGMA) executive chairman and major shareholder Datuk Seri Lee Hock Seng, with 8.21%.

Eonmetall shares were untraded on Friday. The counter last closed at 25 sen on June 4, valuing the company at RM76.95 million.  

Read also:
Mitraland founder Chuah Theong Yee emerges as substantial shareholder of Eonmetall

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Ecomate to diversify into ICT solutions, undertake bonus issue
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Furniture maker Ecomate Holdings Bhd (KL:ECOMATE) said the company plans to diversify into ICT solutions on the back of the acquisition of a majority stake in a computer servicing firm.

The company inked an agreement on Friday to buy a 60% stake in Progressive Computer Systems Sdn Bhd (PCS) from the firm's sole shareholder, Law Seng Peng, for RM8.4 million, according to Ecomate's bourse filing.

Attached to the deal is a profit guarantee by Law that PCS will post profits after tax of at least RM3 million for the financial years ending Feb 28, 2026 (FY2026) to FY2028. Any deficiencies will be paid by the vendor to Ecomate.

“Further, in the event that PCS shall incur loss after taxation of any amount in any financial year within the profit guarantee period, a payment of RM1.8 million, representing 60% of the profit guarantee for each financial year, shall be made in full by the vendor to the company,” said Ecomate.

PCS is principally involved in the marketing and servicing of computers, computer-related accessories, and peripherals, said Ecomate. PCS is also involved in the sale of computer software, software development, and programming services.

For FY2024, PCS’ profit after tax stood at RM2.03 million, down from RM4.8 million in FY2023, but higher than RM2.01 million in FY2022. Revenue rose to RM22.65 million in FY2024 from RM20.02 million in FY2023 and RM20.22 million in FY2022.

Ecomate said the purchase consideration of RM8.4 million represents a price-to-earnings ratio of 2.8 times based on the profit guarantee.
 
Given the profit guarantee, Ecomate anticipates the ICT business may contribute at least 25% of the company’s earnings or result in a diversion of at least a quarter of its net assets in securing additional projects to expand the segment.

Bonus issues of shares and warrants

Meanwhile, Ecomate also announced its plans to undertake a bonus issue of up to 358.03 million shares on the basis of one bonus share for every one existing share, as well as a separate bonus issue of 358.03 million warrants on the same basis.

The exercise price of the warrants is to be determined later. However, for illustrative purposes, Ecomate said the warrants are expected to raise RM222 million when fully exercised.

Proceeds will be used to fund the group’s working capital requirements, it noted.

Shares in Ecomate ended unchanged at RM1.29, valuing the company at RM461.85 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: UK weighs lifting retail ban on crypto exchange-traded products
data.description:
(June 6): The UK’s financial regulator proposed lifting its ban on retail investors buying exchange-traded products (ETPs) tied to cryptocurrencies, seeking to bolster competitiveness against a resurgent US crypto market under Donald Trump.

The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) said the change would mean existing exchange-traded notes tied to tokens like bitcoin and ether can be sold to retail buyers, as long as they’re traded on an FCA-approved exchange. The regulator permitted such products to be traded on venues like the London Stock Exchange last year, but only among professional investors.

While crypto ETPs have been available on European bourses for a decade, the market has been quickly overtaken by similar US products that were first introduced early last year. Spot crypto ETFs traded in the US now oversee more than US$130 billion (RM549.9 billion) — roughly eight times the total value of European ETPs. 

“This consultation demonstrates our commitment to supporting the growth and competitiveness of the UK’s crypto industry,” David Geale, the FCA’s executive director of payments and digital assets, said in a statement Friday. 

The FCA is consulting on broader rules to regulate the cryptocurrency space, with a regime expected to be in place next year. The watchdog’s ban on retail access to other cryptocurrency derivatives will remain in place, it said.

Uploaded by Chng Shear Lane

data.title: Taiwan's Pegatron is in final stage of evaluating US factory plan
data.description:
TAIPEI (June 6): Key Apple and Dell supplier Pegatron is in the final stage of evaluating its US factory plan, with a decision set to be unveiled this month or the next, its president and CEO, Kuang-Chih Cheng, said on Friday.

Key considerations for the Taiwan company include land and labour costs, with electricity being the most critical factor for artificial intelligence (AI) server production, Cheng told reporters.

"Like many of our peers and competitors, the areas we’re considering are likely to be similar," he said in response to a query about potential locations, speaking on the sidelines of an annual shareholders' meeting.

Taiwan manufacturers Foxconn, Inventec and Wistron have recently announced expansion plans in the US state of Texas.

Pegatron has been diversifying manufacturing sites away from China since US President Donald Trump's first term, expanding into Southeast Asia and Mexico. It already has a maintenance base in Indiana and an office in California.

The company began planning its AI server production line in Mexico last year, and mass production is set to begin in the third quarter, Cheng added.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Omesti plans share issuances to settle debt and capital reduction to slash accumulated losses
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Information and communication technology services group Omesti Bhd (KL:OMESTI) has announced a series of exercises aimed at settling part of its debt, raising funds, and reducing its accumulated losses.

They include share settlement exercises to part-settle debt owed to the group's 25.77%-owned associate Microlink Bhd (KL:MICROLN) and outstanding redeemable preference shares (RPS), as well as a RM62.12 million rights issue with free warrants, a RM45.65 million private placement and a RM200 million capital reduction exercise, according to the company’s bourse filing on Friday.

The debt settlement with Microlink involves Omesti issuing up to 85 million new shares to the listed associate at a minimum of 10 sen each.

As at June 5, Omesti owed Microlink RM30.03 million. Based on an indicative price of 10 sen per share, the debt settlement will offset RM8.5 million of the debt, trimming the outstanding debt owed to RM21.53 million.

Meanwhile, Omesti plans to settle RM106.44 million in RPS via the issuance of up to 1.06 billion new shares at a minimum issue price of 10 sen apiece.

The proposed RPS settlement is deemed a related-party transaction given major shareholder Datuk Mah Siew Kwok holds 19.49% of the RPS, while deputy chairman and substantial shareholder Datuk Seri Thong Kok Khee is deemed interested in 10.55% of the RPS via Insas Bhd (KL:INSAS)

Subsequently, Omesti plans to undertake a rights issue of up to 789.04 million shares on the basis of one new share for every one share held, and up to 315.61 warrants on the basis of two warrants for every five rights shares subscribed

Based on an indicative issue price of eight sen per share, the rights issue is envisioned to raise up to RM63.12 million in proceeds. Of the amount, RM40.51 million will be used to fund the group’s project, while the remainder will be for repayment of bank borrowings, working capital of its healthcare division, and defraying the exercises’ costs.

Next, Omesti will implement a private placement of up to 456.47 million new shares, or 15% of the enlarged share base following the conclusion of the prior exercises to third-party Bumiputera investors at a price to be determined later.

At an illustrative issue price of 10 sen per share, the private placement is expected to raise RM45.65 million. The proceeds will be used to repay the outstanding RM21.53 million in debt owed to Microlink, while the remainder will be used to fund working capital.

Lastly, Omesti plans to undertake a reduction of its issued share capital by RM200 million and use the corresponding credit against its accumulated losses.

Based on its accumulated losses as at end-March of RM286.72 million and including the estimated costs of the exercises, the group’s accumulated losses will be reduced to RM88.52 million.

All in all, assuming full exercise of all outstanding warrants and maximum scenarios of each proposed exercise, the exercises will enlarge Omesti’s share base to 3.5 billion shares from the current 540.67 million.

According to Omesti, it has not undertaken a cash call in the past 12 months.

Shares in Omesti ended half a sen or 4.55% lower at 10.5 sen, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM54.19 million. Microlink closed unchanged at 17.5 sen, valuing the company at RM182.71 million.

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Baltic Exchange shipping updates: June 6, 2025
data.description:
A weekly round-up of tanker and dry bulk market (June 6, 2025)

This report is produced by the Baltic Exchange.

The Baltic Exchange, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Singapore Exchange, is the world's only independent source of maritime market information for the trading and settlement of physical and derivative contracts.

Its international community of over 650 members encompasses the majority of world shipping interests and commits to a code of business conduct overseen by the Baltic.

For daily freight market reports and assessments, please visit www.balticexchange.com.

Capesize 

The Capesize market enjoyed a strong upward trajectory this week, driven by consistent gains in the Pacific and mounting support in both South Brazil and the North Atlantic. The BCI 5TC rose steadily from US$19,071 on Monday to US$23,572 by week’s end, reflecting robust demand and tightening tonnage across both basins. In the Pacific, C5 rates climbed from below US$9 to US$10.40-US$10.50 levels, underpinned by limited tonnage, strong iron ore demand, and increased activity from miners and operators. The South Brazil and West Africa to China (C3) markets gained traction midweek, supported by Vale’s presence and a tightening list of ballasters, lifting rates from the low US$21s to the mid US$24s. The North Atlantic, initially quiet, gathered momentum midweek, with firming trans-Atlantic and EC Canada to China fixtures boosting sentiment and rates. 

Panamax 

The Panamax market wrapped up a notably active week with solid gains, particularly driven by renewed strength in the Atlantic. The North Atlantic led the charge with significant rate improvements, while South America added further support. A tightening tonnage list mid-week helped lift sentiment, with fixtures reflecting the firmer tone: an 82,000dwt was fixed delivery Gibraltar for a trip via NC South America, redelivery Gibraltar-Barcelona range at US$11,000, and another 82,000dwt fixed delivery EC India for an EC South America fronthaul at US$13,000. In Asia, the week was more mixed. The pull from EC South America helped reinforce owners’ sentiment, especially for open tonnage in Southeast Asia. However, rates for longer Pacific rounds — particularly ex-NoPac and Australia — softened into the US$8,000s before showing signs of recovery towards the week’s end. Period activity remained subdued, though there was a notable report of a newbuilding 82,000dwt delivery ex-yard China fixing basis one year at US$13,000.

Ultramax/Supramax 

It was another challenging week for the sector, with rates in both the Atlantic and Pacific regions under continued downward pressure. In the Continent and Mediterranean, sentiment remained largely positional. Fixtures indicated that rates were hovering near last done levels. A 57,000dwt fixed for a delivery Bremen via Kotka to redelivery Indica via COGH at US$13,000. In the South Atlantic and US Gulf, fundamentals remained weak, with an oversupply of tonnage continuing to outpace demand. A 64,000dwt fixed delivery Montevideo around 11/15 June for a trip to redelivery Altamira at US$17,500. The Asian market also experienced a sluggish week, with activity reduced due to regional holidays and subdued sentiment. A 53,000dwt fixed delivery Kandla for a trip to redelivery Vietnam with salt at US$6,500. 

Handysize 

This week, the market delivered a mixed performance across the regions, with overall sentiment remaining flat. In the Continent and Mediterranean, market fundamentals stayed soft, with limited visible activity. A 37,000dwt fixed delivery Skaw for trip to redelivery Portugal with grains at US$9,500. The South Atlantic and US Gulf markets maintained a steady pace, supported by stable tonnage levels and pockets of fresh demand. A 39,000dwt fixed delivery Mobile redelivery Reunion with wood pellets at US$16,000. In Asia, the market also remained flat. Although the tonnage list gradually increased, modest fresh demand helped hold rates steady. No significant shift in cargo volumes was observed to drive rates higher. A 39,000dwt fixed delivery Teluk Bayur via Bunbury to redelivery Nantong with grains at US$12,000. In the period market; a 39,000dwt fixed delivery Caribbeans for 5/7 months redelivery Singapore /Japan range at US$12,650. 

Clean 

LR2 

LR2’s in the MEG have held steadfast this week despite seemingly consistent downward sentiment. The TC1 75kt MEG/Japan index dipped a modest 5.55 points from WS134.72 to WS129.17. A TC20 90kt MEG/UK-Continent also only ticked down US$75,000 to US$3.66 million. 

West of Suez, Mediterranean/East LR2’s saw a couple of market reported fixtures this week however the TC15 index still dropped US$75,000 to US$2.86 million. 

LR1 

MEG LR1’s also only moved a little this week. The TC5 55kt MEG/Japan index hovered around the high WS150’s to low WS160’s all week. A voyage west on TC8 65kt MEG/UK-Continent also floated around the US$2.9 million-US$3 million level. 

On the UK-Continent LR1 freight levels were unwavering for the third week on week this week. The TC16 60kt ARA/West Africa index maintained itself around the WS112-WS113 mark.

MR 

MRs in the MEG were gently pushed down this week. The TC17 35kt MEG/East Africa index as a result went from WS194.64 to WS188.57. This has taken the Baltic round trip TCE for the run to just under US$17,500 /day. 

UK-Continent MRs bottomed out this week. The TC2 37kt ARA/US-Atlantic coast run shed 7.5 points to WS137.5. The TC19 voyage of 37kt ARA/West Africa index match the behaviour of TC2 and went from WS165 to WS156.3. 

USG MRs appeared more balanced this week. The TC14 38kt US-Gulf/UK-Continent trip dropped to WS111.43 from WS129.64 but has returned to WS117.86 at time of writing. The TC18 the 38kt US Gulf/Brazil index came down 10.71 points to WS166.43 and a Caribbean run on TC21, 38kt US-Gulf/Caribbean bottomed out at US$603,214 but is now back up to US$632,143 after starting the week at US$716,429. 

The MR Atlantic Triangulation Basket TCE went from US$25,988 to US$22,732 after bottoming at US$19,857 mid-week. 

Handymax 

The TC6 30kt Cross Mediterranean index dropped sharply this week from WS189.44 to WS135.66. Up on the UK-Continent the TC23 30kt Cross UK-Continent also went from WS183.06 to WS160.56. 

VLCC 

The market plunged further this week, although a floor seems to have been found and rates in the Middle East Gulf and West Africa have arguably started to rebound. The rate for the 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China trip (TD3C) fell overall almost seven points to WS44.35 corresponding to a round-trip TCE of US$24,332. 

In the Atlantic market, the rate for 260,000mt West Africa/China (TD15) is now four points lower than a week ago at WS48.63 giving a round voyage TCE of US$29,631 per day. In the US Gulf region, the interesting story here is that Equinor had a cargo on Wednesday and the two main participants fighting for the business were Exxon and Shell with relets. Exxon won that battle, taking the first counter at US$6,400,000. Since then, sentiment has softened further and the TD22 route of 270,000mt US Gulf to China was last assessed on Thursday at US$6,182,500 (down over US$1 million for the week) which shows a daily round trip TCE of US$27,810. 

Suezmax 

Suezmax owners in the Atlantic region have managed to push rates up. The rate for the 130,000mt Nigeria/UK Continent voyage (TD20) has improved by nearly nine points to WS89.17 meaning a daily round-trip TCE of US$36,509 and the TD27 route (Guyana to UK Continent basis 130,000mt) is similarly assessed almost nine points up for the week at WS86.94 translating to a daily round trip TCE of US$37,793 basis discharge in Rotterdam. The TD6 route of 135,000mt CPC/Augusta is five points firmer at a fraction below WS100 giving a daily TCE of US$36,350. In the Middle East, the rate for the TD23 route of 140,000mt Middle East Gulf to the Mediterranean (via the Suez Canal) remained around the WS83 level.

Aframax

In the North Sea market for the 80,000mt Cross-UK Continent route (TD7) the rate remained at WS122.5 giving a daily round-trip TCE of about US$36,371 basis Hound Point to Wilhelmshaven.

In the Mediterranean, the rate for 80,000mt Cross-Mediterranean (TD19) recovered 15 points to settle around the WS135 mark (basis Ceyhan to Lavera, that shows a daily round trip TCE of a little over US$32,600).

Across the Atlantic, the rollercoaster ride has started again. For the 70,000mt East Coast Mexico/US Gulf route (TD26) the market rose over 40 points but has now fallen back about four points since the peak, at WS171.11 showing a daily round trip TCE of about US$42,200 and the rate for 70,000mt Covenas/US Gulf route (TD9) mirrored the moves of TD26 and is now assessed at WS167.19 giving a daily round-trip TCE of around US$38,700.

The rate for the trans-Atlantic route of 70,000mt US Gulf/UK Continent (TD25) peaked after rising 32 points and then lost eight points on Thursday to be assessed at WS173.33 giving a round trip TCE basis Houston/Rotterdam of US$44,646 per day.

LNG

The LNG market saw muted activity due to most market players attending Nor-Shipping, with slight softening in the Pacific and mixed resilience in the Atlantic basin. Freight rates for both vessel sizes posted losses on the longer Pacific routes, while US-Europe maintained slight upward pressure.

On the BLNG1 Australia-Japan route, rates dropped US$600 for both vessel sizes. The 174k cbm units slipped to US$19,900 per day, while 160k cbm ships settled at US$11,100 per day — continuing the downward trend amid weaker Pacific demand.

In contrast, the Atlantic showed some divergence. BLNG2 US Gulf-Continent saw a modest US$200 gain for 174k cbm vessels to US$29,100 per day, with 160k cbm vessels holding steady at US$14,600 per day, reflecting stable tonnage and modest activity.

However, the BLNG3 US Gulf-Japan route softened further, falling US$800 and US$200 for 174k and 160k cbm vessels respectively, closing at US$34,300 and US$17,500 per day. The decline points to reduced long-haul interest and ongoing pressure from a weak arbitrage window.

The time charter market reflected a cautious tone. Six-month TC rates fell US$300 to US$38,450 per day, and one-year deals edged down US$75 to US$39,800. However, three-year charters rose, climbing US$1,450 to US$56,000 per day.

Despite the mixed signals, sentiment remains steady, with players mostly readying for end of July and August positions.

LPG 

The LPG market posted gains this week across all major routes, despite most players attending Nor-Shipping. A slight widening of the arbitrage helped lift these rates. 

On the BLPG1 Ras Tanura-Chiba route, rates climbed US$0.75 to settle at US$68.75, with TCE earnings rising US$728 to US$54,198/day. 

In the Atlantic, the BLPG2 Houston-Flushing route gained US$2.25, closing at US$64.13, while TCE earnings jumped US$2,604 to US$67,519/day. 

The BLPG3 Houston-Chiba route delivered the strongest growth, gaining US$5.08 to US$119.83, while TCE returns added US$3,918, reaching US$51,372/day. The gains came as vessel availability tightened for long-haul voyages and arbitrage discussions reopened. 

Container

It’s been quite a week in the container market. We’ve seen rates double on some trade lanes as planned increases take effect, with liner companies adjusting services to meet currently high demand.

With the 90-day pause on U.S. import tariffs still in place until August, many shippers are expected to bring forward Q3 and Q4 shipments to beat the deadline. However, given this week’s sharp rate hikes, those who haven’t yet booked may now decide to wait it out. 

We saw FBX01 (China/East Asia – USA West Coast) ending the week at US$6,015/FEU more than double the US$2,770/FEU of last Friday. FBX02 (USA West Coast – China/East Asia) rates remined fairly stable over the week finishing at US$477/FEU. FBX03 (China/East Asia – USA East Coast) jumped US$3,105 over the week ending at US$7,090/FEU. FBX11 (China/East Asia – North Europe), finished the week at US$2,826/FEU, up US$177 on last week. FBX12 (North Europe – China / East Asia) increased to US$488/FEU, up US$93 week on week. FBX13 (China/East Asia – Mediterranean) gained US$1,073 over the week ending at US$4,475/FEU.

Disclaimer:

While reasonable care has been taken by the Baltic Exchange Information Services Limited (BEISL) and The Baltic Exchange (Asia) Pte. Ltd. (BEA, and together with BEISL being Baltic) in providing this information, all such information is for general use, provided without warranty or representation, is not designed to be used for or relied upon for any specific purpose, and does not infringe upon the legitimate rights and interests of any third party including intellectual property. The Baltic will not accept any liability for any loss incurred in any way whatsoever by any person who seeks to rely on the information contained herein.

All intellectual property and related rights in this information are owned by the Baltic. data.title: Former Globetronics CEO Heng Huck Lee joins PIE Industrial's board
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Electronic manufacturing services firm PIE Industrial Bhd (KL:PIE) has appointed former Globetronics Technology Bhd (KL:GTRONIC) chief executive officer Datuk Heng Huck Lee to its board as an independent and non-executive director, with immediate effect.

Heng, 68, who retired as Globetronics CEO in 2022, was also appointed as a member of PIE's nomination and audit committees, PIE's bourse filing Friday showed.

Globetronics is involved in outsourced semiconductor assembly and test and is an original design manufacturer for semiconductor, electronics components and smart electronics devices.

Separately, PIE Industrial announced the departure of Lee Cheow Kooi as its non-independent and non-executive director, and as a member of its nomination and audit committees, “in pursuit of personal interests”. Lee was appointed to the board in November 2016, PIE's annual report showed.

PIE’s net profit jumped 54.8% in the first quarter of 2025, thanks to higher demand from its existing customers and lower provision for slow-moving inventories. Net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 came in at RM15.02 million against RM9.7 million in the same quarter a year ago. Revenue rose 13.6% to RM271.76 million from RM239.18 million.

PIE shares closed six sen or 1.44% lower at RM4.12 on Friday, valuing the company at RM1.58 billion. Year to date, the stock is down by 32%.

Read also:
PIE Industrial’s 1Q earnings surge 55% on higher demand, lower provision for slow-moving inventories

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Gadang faces RM19 mil hit from termination of joint development in Cyberjaya
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Gadang Holdings Bhd (KL:GADANG) announced it will allocate about RM19 million in additional costs for the financial year ending May 31, 2025 (FY2025), following the termination of an agreement to jointly develop a parcel of land in the Cyber 9 precinct of Cyberjaya with Cyberview Sdn Bhd.

The joint development agreement (JDA) was between Gadang's wholly owned Hillstrand Development Sdn Bhd (HDSB) and Cyberview, Cyberjaya's master developer. The agreement, inked in May 2014, was to develop a 121.49 acre plot within 10 years.

The termination is in respect of the undeveloped phases of the project, Gadang said in a bourse filing on Friday. The project comprised four phases, with sub-phases within each phase. All but Phase 4 — comprising sub-phases 4A, 4B and 4C — remained undeveloped.

Gadang said HDSB had previously sought an extension of the implementation schedule to complete the project. Cyberview, in response, wanted an additional return of RM25 million for that, which HDSB was amenable to if the requisite planning approval to revise Phase 4C from commercial shop lots to serviced apartments could be obtained, which would support the proposed additional returns.

But the parties could not secure the necessary regulatory approvals, which led to the mutual termination of the JDA.

Gadang said HDSB had incurred RM40 million in development expenditure for the uncompleted Phase 4, and the joint venture parties had agreed to cap Cyberview's portion of the shared costs payable to HDSB at RM21 million.

The RM19 million provision will be reflected in Gadang's fourth quarter results for FY2025, which are expected to be announced in July.

Gadang shares closed unchanged at 25 sen on Friday, giving the construction firm a market capitalisation of RM200.22 million. Year-to-date, the stock has fallen by 25.4%.

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Annuar Zaini named new chairman of 7-Eleven Malaysia
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Tan Sri Mohd Annuar Zaini has been appointed as the new independent and non-executive chairman of 7-Eleven Malaysia Holdings Bhd (KL:SEM), effective immediately.

He replaces Datuk Farhash Wafa Salvador, who stepped down from the role on May 30 to pursue other interests.

According to 7-Eleven Malaysia’s filing with Bursa Malaysia, Mohd Annuar, 73, holds a bachelor's degree in economics and a master’s in law and diplomacy.

He began his public service career in 1977, serving in various ministries, federal departments, and the Perak state government, before opting for early retirement in 1999,

Since then, he has sat on the boards of several public-listed companies, private firms, and government-linked agencies. These include Berjaya Corporation Bhd (KL:BJCORP) as executive director (2001–2004), Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) as chairman from February 2004 to January 2010, and PLUS Expressways Bhd as a board member from 2007 to 2012.

He currently also serves as chairman of UDA Holdings Bhd, a government-linked company under the Ministry of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives, which is tasked with urban infrastructure development.

Additionally, Mohd Annuar is a board member of the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso), chairman of Country Annex Sdn Bhd and MRCB Land Sdn Bhd, and senior adviser for international affairs at the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).

Shares in 7-Eleven Malaysia closed unchanged at RM1.98 on Friday, valuing the group at RM2.32 billion.

Read also:
Farhash Wafa Salvador resigns as 7-Eleven Malaysia chairman

 

Edited ByS Kanagaraju

data.title: Bursa Malaysia fines Meridian CEO, directors RM350,000 for late disclosure of minimal operations
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Bursa Malaysia has reprimanded Meridian Bhd (KL:MERIDIAN) and fined five directors a total of RM350,000 for failing to promptly disclose that the company had minimal business activity.

Meridian’s fourth quarter 2023 report, released on Aug 29, 2023, showed a revenue of RM2.74 million — only 0.93% of its RM294 million share capital — well below the 5% threshold that requires an announcement under Bursa Malaysia rules. 

In a filing with the bourse, the regulator said the company only made the required disclosure on Sept 26, 2023, after being contacted by Bursa Malaysia a day earlier, resulting in a 28-day delay.

The company secretary had reminded the board about this requirement during meetings in May and August, but no action was taken. The regulator said Meridian and its directors had no valid reason to assume revenue would rise from 0.93% to meet the 5% threshold by November 2023. In reality, revenue for that period was only RM3.796 million, or 1.29% of share capital.

CEO Datuk Yap Ting Hau and director Tang Boon Koon were each fined RM100,000, while three independent directors — Mark Chew, Ng Kok Hok, and Kunamony Kandiah — were each fined RM50,000.

Bursa Malaysia said the failure was serious, as such information helps investors assess if the company can remain listed. It added that changing the financial year-end did not remove Meridian’s obligation to make the announcement and reminded the board to maintain good governance and transparency.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Ancom Nylex seeks to take full control of delisted Nylex via capital reduction
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (June 6): Following Nylex (M) Bhd's delisting from Bursa Malaysia in March, the cash company’s major shareholder Ancom Nylex Bhd (KL:ANCOMNY) is seeking to take full control of the company through a selective capital reduction and repayment exercise.

Entitled shareholders will collectively receive a capital repayment of RM5.3 million, or 5.1 sen per share, in cash under the exercise proposed by Ancom Nylex and its wholly owned unit Rhodemark Development Sdn Bhd, according to the letter of offer on Friday.  

Ancom Nylex and Rhodemark, which together control 42.21% of Nylex, will be the sole shareholders of Nylex at the conclusion of the exercise.

The offer price of 5.1 sen  for the remaining 103.89 million shares, or 57.79% stake, is based on Nylex’s net assets of RM16.96 million minus deferred tax assets of RM7.79 million divided by its share base of 179.79 million.

“The proposed share capital reduction is expected to be funded via the internal funds of Nylex,” said Ancom Nylex and Rhodemark.

Persons acting in concert (PACs) who hold a collective 0.33% stake in Nylex will participate in the exercise as entitled shareholders to consolidate the ownership of the company under the two major shareholders.

These PACs comprise Datuk Siew Ka Wei and his family, Tan Sri Mohamad Fuzi Harun, Low Huoi Seong, Rizainal Mustaffa, and Asmariah Ismail. Siew is the group managing director of Nylex as well as major shareholder and executive vice chairman of Ancom.

In explaining the rationale behind the proposal, Ancom Nylex and Rhodemark said it would enable the pair to have greater control over the company’s business plans. The company had 12,145 shareholders as at end-May.

The plan would also present an opportunity for entitled shareholders to immediately realise their investments as well as return the company’s capital resources to shareholders in a timely manner, said Ancom Nylex and Rhodemark.

Nylex was delisted on March 11 after its bid for a further extension to submit its regularisation plan was dismissed by Bursa Securities.

It was classified as a Practice Note 17 company after it divested all of its assets and liabilities to Ancom Nylex, then known as Ancom Bhd, for RM179.3 million in a cash-plus-share deal in January 2022.

Shares in Ancom Nylex ended half a sen or 0.53% higher at 95 sen, valuing the company at RM1.11 billion.

Read also:
Nylex to be delisted on March 11

 

Edited ByS Kanagaraju
